title: What is Contamination
wikitext:
[]: What is Contamination?             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Sun Aug 24, 2008 9:25 pm I ran a search. Really, I did. But that search engine thing confuddles me. So I'm just gonna ask.. What is contamination? For example, in episode 12 they're running harmonics tests (whatever that is) and while Rei and Asuka reached their limit, Shinji's could go "deeper". Despite my best attempts to read around and try to figure this out, I've always been confused. Also, the mention of "plug depth" seems to have something to do something with contamination. They, of course, use this word throughout the series. The other examples seem to make sense, like the contamination of Ireul. Obviously, they mean that they don't want Ireul "infecting" the Evas. Or when Arael contaminates Asuka's mind. Again, this implies some sort of infiltration by the Angels. But I'm not understanding what's being contaminated if they're just running tests. Some clarification would be much appreciated. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Aug 24, 2008 10:05 pm Plug depth refers to how far into the EVA's core the entry plug has been inserted.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Aug 25, 2008 12:01 am Contamination: Etymology: Middle English, from Latin contaminatus, past participle of contaminare; akin to Latin contingere to have contact with The last part. They could be using that word in regards to having full contact with the eva and related eva systems. Another definition of contamination is "to make unfit for use by the introduction of unwholesome or undesirable elements." Since an evangelion's LCL is foreign to a human body, the LCL will contaminate the pilot, which acts as a catalyst for eva operation and feeds oxygen to the pilot. And the more LCL a pilot can withstand, the more in sync the pilot is with the eva. This is surely where "plug depth" comes from, because the LCL is probably more dense and viscous at a lower part of the plug, which as the pilot moves further down, the higher their sync ratio. However, this definition can also refer to the pilot themselves contaminating the eva, buy having their soul enter into the eva alongside its existing resident soul. Well, I really let it all out on that one. That is just my theory, so please, take it with a grain of salt. Source: [url]http://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/contaminating[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by p06 » Mon Aug 25, 2008 3:59 am plug depth sounds like a sexual reference to me -_- as for contamination, what was the original Japanese word used? it sounded like problematic translation to me.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Mon Aug 25, 2008 4:44 am a perfect example of a full contamination was shown in 19th episode "Introjection", "Otoko no tatakai (Man's Battle)" (男の戰い) when Shinji turns into soup. From the mentioned synch test in ep 12 we know that 'contamination' is what happenes if they force excessive synchronization. In episode 19, Shinji synchronizes a little to much and voilà .

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Aug 25, 2008 9:04 am So what I said may be correct? To an extent?

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Mon Aug 25, 2008 10:31 am I would think that contamination is anything that interferes with synchronization. It could be mental contamination, like with Asuka in episode 22, or physical contamination, like with Rei in episode 23 (I think; it was the one where tentacles were involved).

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Aug 25, 2008 1:13 pm That is one type of contamination, yes, but that is not the type of contamination that AsukaChan is referring to, I don't think.

[]:             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Mon Aug 25, 2008 2:03 pm I'm referring to whatever sort of contamination it was that made Shinji's synch test scores higher than Rei's and Asuka's. I just remember Ritsuko talking and saying that while the girls met their limit Shinji could go take a bit more. Re-watching the scene made me think that maybe they meant that there is a limit at which point the sychronization creates some sort of mental disturbance in the pilot. Then I watched the one where they were doing the test with Shinji in Eva-00 and that's when Ritsuko mentions "plug depth" and exclaims that the sort of disturbance they were getting that was causing the Eva to go nuts couldn't happen at that plug depth. Does that make it clearer, maybe?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Aug 25, 2008 4:55 pm So basically, as the pilot goes deeper in the plug, they become more contaminated with LCL, causing their sync ration to become higher. And because the pilot is being "contaminated" it becomes harder to be at such depths. Also, if the plug is indeed connected to the core, the deeper the pilot goes, the closer they are to the eva's resident soul, which would explain the higher sync ratio.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Aug 25, 2008 5:11 pm I think the truth here is a mixture of both Action_Bastard and Gamer's ideas. EDIT: This probably belongs in discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Aug 25, 2008 10:20 pm I kind of assume "contamination" means "the point at which your mind pattern no longer harmonizes with the Eva's, and ends up just making "neural bleed-back" (as Farscape calls it) and "static"

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Aug 25, 2008 10:37 pm CorporalChaos wrote: EDIT: This probably belongs in discussion.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Aug 26, 2008 5:23 am Perhaps plug depth refers not to the level of harmonic synchronisation between eva and pilot, but to the intensity of the impulses between them? Well, besides from the obvious "it litterally goes deeper into the plug/core". I would assume contamination is anything that gets somewhere that it shouldn't be, whether it comes from some random git being in the cockpit messing with synchronization or from an angel using some crazy ATF voodoo.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Aug 26, 2008 7:06 am It's pretty straightforward. Synchronisation is the merging of two minds, the more they are merged the higher the synch ratio and the higher the mental contamination index (the graph of which is shown I think in #12 like you say). Once that goes pass a certain point mental contamination becomes bio contamination, where the merging of two minds becomes the merging of two bodies. Beyond that lies reverting back into LCL as in #19 and E.O.E. with the complete merging of both individuals, so you "don't know where you end and others begin", or in the case of E.O.E. of everyone. The above is one of the themes in #25 and #26 and is talked about clearly, so it might be worth going back to those two episodes for more information about why synchronisation can occur in the first place and all the rest of it. The odd thing about the harmonics tests is that there's talk of synchronisation when the pilots are nowhere near their Evangelions - this leads on to the question of why the EVAs can generate AT Fields when the pilot isn't onboard and why that seems necessary, along with the MAGI, for the tests with the simulation bodies to work in #13 and whether or not the same mechanic is being used during the harmonics tests to remotely hook the pilots up with their EVAs.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Wed Aug 27, 2008 12:05 am CorporalChaos wrote: I think the truth here is a mixture of both Action_Bastard and Gamer's ideas.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Aug 27, 2008 12:16 am Of course contamination will aid syncing. Without the LCL, a foreign substance to the human body, there would be no syncing. Adding on to my theory, one must remember the old saying: a little bit goes a long way, and a lot of a good thing can be bad. The deeper you are in the plug, the higher the sync ratio and the closer you are to merging with the resident soul and the eva itself. But, as the pilot becomes subject to further contamination, the sync ratio will inevitably go higher, causing a shift of the sync graph to the negative spectrum, causing the pilot to merge further with the other soul, reverting them into LCL. So in essence, our theories can be merged into one solid theorem.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Wed Aug 27, 2008 12:18 am See, I was thinking more along the lines of "synching with something other than your Eva"; generally the "something other" would be an angel, and would require detaching or destruction of the affected part.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Aug 27, 2008 12:21 am Can you please elaborate?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Aug 27, 2008 7:54 am simon wrote: a perfect example of a full contamination was shown in 19th episode "Introjection", "Otoko no tatakai (Man's Battle)" (男の戰い) when Shinji turns into soup.
